```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  x <- sample(1:12,4,replace=TRUE)
  am <- mean(x); gm <- prod(x)^(1/length(x)); hm <- length(x)/sum(1/x)
  vals <- round(c(hm,gm,am,mean(1/x),sum(x)),4)
  if (length(unique(x)) > 1L && length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
xt <- paste(x,collapse=", ")
```

Question
========
Para os números positivos $(`r xt`)$, qual é a média harmônica?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A média harmônica é
$$HM=\frac{n}{\sum_i1/x_i}=`r sprintf("%.4f",hm)`.$$
Além disso, Jensen permite estabelecer $HM\leq GM\leq AM$; aqui $GM=`r sprintf("%.4f",gm)`$ e $AM=`r sprintf("%.4f",am)`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S10Q9_medias_harmonica_geometrica
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
